do.result: The grid automatically adjusts the number of columns based on available space! Cards are always equal height even with different content lengths.
do.proofRequired: Screenshots at 3 different widths showing different column counts.

concepts.name: The magic grid line: auto-fit + minmax()
concepts.definition: grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr)) creates fully responsive grids in ONE line. auto-fit = as many columns as fit. minmax(280px, 1fr) = min 280px, max equal share.
concepts.analogy: A smart bookshelf that automatically adjusts the number of books per row based on how wide the shelf is.

concepts.name: Equal height cards with Flexbox
concepts.definition: Cards use display: flex + flex-direction: column. Setting flex: 1 on card-content makes it grow to fill available space, so all cards match height regardless of content length.
concepts.analogy: Like adjustable shelves — the content area stretches to make all cards the same height.

concepts.name: Hover effects with transform
concepts.definition: transform: translateY(-8px) lifts the card up on hover. Combined with a growing box-shadow, it creates a "lifting off the page" effect. transition: all 0.3s ease makes it smooth.
concepts.analogy: Picking up a card from a table — it rises and its shadow gets bigger.

concepts.name: No media queries needed
concepts.definition: auto-fit + minmax() handles responsiveness automatically. The browser calculates how many 280px columns fit and creates that many. No breakpoints required!
concepts.analogy: A smart container that figures out the best arrangement on its own, like water flowing to fill any shape.

understand.keyTakeaway: auto-fit + minmax() creates responsive grids without media queries. Combine with Flexbox for equal-height cards.

build.hint: JavaScript toggles the .hidden class on cards based on which filter button is clicked. The CSS grid automatically adjusts when cards disappear!

challenge.title: Create Your Portfolio Gallery
challenge.mission: Build a portfolio gallery with YOUR projects (real or imagined).
requirements: At least 6 project cards with image, title, description, and category tag
Responsive grid (1-4 columns depending on screen width)
challenge.starterCode:
<!-- Use the gallery code from Task 1 as your starting point.
     Replace placeholder images and text with your own projects.
     Add filter buttons from Task 2.
     Customize colors to match your personal brand. -->
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a "Load More" button that reveals additional hidden cards, or add a modal popup when clicking a card!

capabilities: Create responsive card galleries with CSS Grid
Use auto-fit and minmax for automatic column counts

bridge.preview: In Lesson 3.5, you'll learn to make text and spacing scale perfectly across devices using modern CSS techniques like clamp() and CSS variables!
